tossups.question: Note to players: composer and type of piece required. The middle movement of the fourth of these pieces opens with solo marimba playing in 19/8 20/8 time. A bar of messy glissando is followed by the marking “continue” over a crescendo at the start of one of these pieces inspired by (+) Hinduism. The penultimate one of these pieces features a second movement “love song” to the composer’s wife Hinako. The father of another wife of the composer conducted the Birmingham Symphony, which commissioned one of these works depicting “a million lights.” “Lament,” “Conflict,” and “Triumph” are the three movements of the first of these pieces, which memorializes a genocide. A solo flute bridges the opening “dawn theme” and a depiction of an (*) eruption in the final movement of one of these pieces. The most famous of these pieces is the second, nicknamed Mysterious Mountain. For 10 points, name this set of 67 orchestral works by a mountain-obsessed 20th-century Armenian-American composer.
tossups.answer: symphonies by Alan Hovhaness [accept Alan Vaness Chakmakjian instead of Alan Hovhaness] (The first and fourth sentences refer to No. 22, City of Light.)
tossups.answer_sanitized: symphonies by Alan Hovhaness [accept Alan Vaness Chakmakjian instead of Alan Hovhaness] (The first and fourth sentences refer to No. 22, City of Light.)
tossups.question_sanitized: Note to players: composer and type of piece required. The middle movement of the fourth of these pieces opens with solo marimba playing in 19/8 20/8 time. A bar of messy glissando is followed by the marking "continue" over a crescendo at the start of one of these pieces inspired by (+) Hinduism. The penultimate one of these pieces features a second movement "love song" to the composer's wife Hinako. The father of another wife of the composer conducted the Birmingham Symphony, which commissioned one of these works depicting "a million lights." "Lament," "Conflict," and "Triumph" are the three movements of the first of these pieces, which memorializes a genocide. A solo flute bridges the opening "dawn theme" and a depiction of an (*) eruption in the final movement of one of these pieces. The most famous of these pieces is the second, nicknamed Mysterious Mountain. For 10 points, name this set of 67 orchestral works by a mountain-obsessed 20th-century Armenian-American composer.

bonuses.leadin: In one composer's fiftieth symphony, one of these places is evoked in the allegro second movement nicknamed "Spirit Lake." For 10 points each:
parts: Name these places, a "Mysterious" one of which titles a symphony by an Armenian American composer. Woodwinds glissando between held A's and higher notes in a piece named for one of these places depicting a witches' Sabbath on St. John's Eve.

This Armenian American composer wrote the symphonies Mount St. Helens, To the Appalachian Mountains, and Mysterious Mountain. This composer's first symphony, Exile, commemorates the Armenian Genocide.

Hovhaness's Symphony No. 66 is titled for one of these pieces to Glacial Peak. A section of Finlandia titled for one of these devotional songs for prayer pulls from Christian examples including "Be still, my soul."
answers: mountains [or The Mysterious Mountain; or Night on Bald Mountain; or Mount St. Helens]
answers_sanitized: mountains [or The Mysterious Mountain; or Night on Bald Mountain; or Mount St. Helens]
